commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1413742b808ea1b119fe3d8efaf88baa4e63b3ba)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wires Control UI sidebar and chat session picker `sessions.list` requests to ask for derived titles, adds a UI row type field, and tests display fallback behavior.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +49. Total +56 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the PR can request `derivedTitle`, but picker option labels still route through `resolveSessionScopedOptionLabel`, which ignores rows whose only friendly field is `derivedTitle`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual follow-up is needed because the known fix and proof live on a separate branch while the actual PR head remains incomplete and exact-head proof must come from the contributor or maintainer.

target.title: feat #94529: auto-derive session title in sidebar
target.body:
## Summary

Control UI session lists now request derived titles from the gateway and fall back to them when a session has no user-set label or display name. This makes the sidebar and chat session picker distinguishable after opening multiple sessions.

## Root Cause

`sessions.list` already supports `includeDerivedTitles`, but the Control UI frontend never requested it, so the sidebar showed generic labels such as "New Session" for fresh sessions.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts`: include `includeDerivedTitles: true` in sidebar session list requests.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts`: include `includeDerivedTitles: true` in chat session picker requests.
- `ui/src/ui/types.ts`: add `derivedTitle?: string` to `GatewaySessionRow`.
- `ui/src/ui/session-display.ts`: use `derivedTitle` as a fallback after explicit `label` and `displayName`.

## Regression Test Plan

- Updated `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` to assert `includeDerivedTitles: true` in expected `sessions.list` params.
- Updated `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` for chat session picker params.
- Added `resolveSessionDisplayName` tests in `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` covering `derivedTitle` fallback and precedence over explicit labels.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI sidebar and chat session picker showed generic "New Session" labels for fresh sessions instead of derived titles.

Real environment tested: Linux development checkout running the Control UI test harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --run
```

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ ui/ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts (78 tests)
✓ ui/ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts (77 tests)
✓ unit-ui/ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts (110 tests)

Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 265 passed (265)
```

These tests verify that the frontend now requests `includeDerivedTitles: true` and that `derivedTitle` is used as a fallback display name when no explicit label or displayName is set.

Observed result after fix: `sessions.list` calls from the sidebar and chat session picker include `includeDerivedTitles: true`; `resolveSessionDisplayName` returns the derived title when no explicit label/displayName exists.

What was not tested: A live browser screenshot of the Control UI sidebar rendering multiple sessions with distinct derived titles (requires a running gateway and browser). Requesting maintainer `proof:` override for the missing live UI screenshot.

Closes #94529.
